subset t ⊆ s that is almost everywhere equal to s
  have ⟨t, t_sub_s, t_m, t_eq_s⟩ := h.exists_measurable_subset_ae_eq
  -- Use t as the required subset
  use t, t_sub_s, t_m
  constructor
  · -- Prove that volume t = volume s using the fact that t and s are almost everywhere equal
    exact measure_congr t_eq_s
  · -- Prove that volume (s \ t) = 0 using the fact that t and s are almost everywhere equal
    exact ae_le_set.mp t_eq_s.symm.le

s, MeasurableSet t ∧ volume t = volume s ∧ volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
    have ⟨t, t_sub_s, t_m, t_eq_s⟩ := h.exists_measurable_subset_ae_eq
    refine' ⟨t, t_sub_s, t_m, _, _⟩
    · -- Prove that the measure of t is equal to the measure of s
      have h₂ : volume t = volume s := by
        have h₃ : volume t = volume s := by
          -- Use the fact that t and s are equal almost everywhere to show their measures are equal
          apply measure_congr_ae
          exact t_eq_s
        exact h₃
      exact h₂
    · -- Prove that the measure of the difference s \ t is zero
      have h₂ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
        have h₃ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
          -- Use the fact that t and s are equal almost everywhere to show the difference has measure zero
          have h₄ : (s \ t : Set ℝ) ⊆ {x : ℝ | False} := by
            intro x hx
            have h₅ : x ∈ s := hx.1
            have h₆ : x ∉ t := hx.2
            have h₇ : x ∈ s := h₅
            have h₈ : x ∉ t := h₆
            have h₉ : x ∈ t := by
              -- Since t and s are equal almost everywhere, x must be in t
              have h₁₀ : t =ᵐ[volume] s := by
                simpa [Filter.eventually_eq] using t_eq_s.symm
              have h₁₁ : x ∈ s := h₅
              have h₁₂ : x ∈ t := by
                -- Use the almost everywhere equality to conclude x ∈ t
                have h₁₃ : x ∈ t := by
                  by_contra h₁₄
                  have h₁₅ : x ∉ t := h₁₄
                  have h₁₆ : x ∈ s := h₅
                  have h₁₇ : x ∉ t := h₁₅
                  have h₁₈ : x ∈ s \ t := ⟨h₁₆, h₁₇⟩
                  have h₁₉ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
                    have h₂₀ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
                      -- Since t and s are equal almost everywhere, s \ t has measure zero
                      have h₂₁ : (s \ t : Set ℝ) ⊆ {x : ℝ | False} := by
                        intro x hx
                        have h₂₂ : x ∈ s := hx.1
                        have h₂₃ : x ∉ t := hx.2
                        have h₂₄ : x ∈ s := h₂₂
                        have h₂₅ : x ∉ t := h₂₃
                        have h₂₆ : x ∈ t := by
                          -- Since t and s are equal almost everywhere, x must be in t
                          have h₂₇ : t =ᵐ[volume] s := by
                            simpa [Filter.eventually_eq] using t_eq_s.symm
                          have h₂₈ : x ∈ s := h₂₄
                          have h₂₉ : x ∈ t := by
                            -- Use the almost everywhere equality to conclude x ∈ t
                            filter_upwards [h₂₇] with y hy
                            by_cases h₃₀ : y = x
                            · simp_all
                            · simp_all
                          exact h₂₉
                        contradiction
                      have h₃₁ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
                        calc
                          volume (s \ t) ≤ volume {x : ℝ | False} := by
                            apply volume_mono
                            exact h₂₁
                          _ = 0 := by simp
                      exact h₃₁
                    exact h₂₀
                  have h₃₂ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := h₁₉
                  have h₃₃ : volume (s \ t) > 0 := by
                    -- Since x ∈ s \ t, the measure of s \ t is positive
                    have h₃₄ : (x : ℝ) ∈ s \ t := ⟨h₁₆, h₁₇⟩
                    have h₃₅ : 0 < volume (s \ t) := by
                      -- Use the fact that x is in s \ t and the measure is positive
                      have h₃₆ : 0 < volume (s \ t) := by
                        -- Use the fact that x is in s \ t and the measure is positive
                        apply (volume_pos_iff (s \ t) (by
                          -- Show that s \ t is measurable
                          have h₃₇ : MeasurableSet (s \ t) := by
                            -- Since t is measurable and s is measurable, s \ t is measurable
                            have h₃₈ : MeasurableSet s := by
                              -- Since t is measurable and t ⊆ s, s is measurable
                              have h₃₉ : MeasurableSet t := t_m
                              have h₄₀ : MeasurableSet s := by
                                -- Since t is measurable and t ⊆ s, s is measurable
                                have h₄₁ : MeasurableSet t := t_m
                                have h₄₂ : MeasurableSet s := by
                                  -- Since t is measurable and t ⊆ s, s is measurable
                                  aesop
                                exact h₄₂
                              exact h₄₀
                            have h₄₃ : MeasurableSet t := t_m
                            -- Since t is measurable and s is measurable, s \ t is measurable
                            have h₄₄ : MeasurableSet (s \ t) := by
                              -- Use the fact that the difference of measurable sets is measurable
                              apply MeasurableSet.sdiff
                              exact h₃₈
                              exact h₄₃
                            exact h₄₄
                          exact h₃₇
                        )).mpr
                        exact ⟨x, by
                          -- Show that x is in s \ t
                          simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
                          ⟩
                      exact h₃₆
                    exact h₃₅
                  linarith
                simp_all
              exact h₁₃
            contradiction
          -- Since s \ t is a subset of the empty set, its measure is zero
          have h₅ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
            calc
              volume (s \ t) ≤ volume {x : ℝ | False} := by
                apply volume_mono
                exact h₄
              _ = 0 := by simp
          exact h₅
        exact h₃
      exact h₂
  exact h₁

s \ t =ᵐ[volume] ∅ := by
          -- Use the given equality condition to establish that s \ t is almost everywhere empty
          have h₅ : t =ᵐ[volume] s := by
            filter_upwards [t_eq_s] with x hx using hx
          have h₆ : s \ t =ᵐ[volume] ∅ := by
            filter_upwards [h₅] with x hx using by
              by_cases hx' : x ∈ s
              · -- Case: x ∈ s
                simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
                <;> aesop
              · -- Case: x ∉ s
                simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
                <;> aesop
          exact h₆
        -- Since s \ t is almost everywhere empty, its volume is 0
        have h₅ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
          have h₆ : volume (s \ t) = 0 := by
            rw [MeasureTheory.volume_eq_zero]
            filter_upwards [h₄] with x hx using by
              simp_all [Set.mem_diff]
              <;> aesop
          exact h₆
        exact h₅
      exact h₃
    exact h₁
